title: haplophyllum molle
intro: Haplophyllum molle, also known as dwarf gorse, is a small, thorny shrub native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the Rutaceae family of plants. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as dyspepsia and diarrhea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Haplophyllum molle include its ability to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve overall gut health. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
